name: sigwire
desc: tail -f for signals. Every signal any process on the box raises — who sent it, who it hit, which signal, how it was raised (kill(2), the kernel, a POSIX timer)…
descFull: tail -f for signals. Every signal any process on the box raises — who sent it, who it hit, which signal, how it was raised (kill(2), the kernel, a POSIX timer), whether the target caught it and how long its handler ran, whether it tore a blocked syscall out with EINTR.

hnPosts.title: Show HN: Sigwire – a live TUI switchboard for every signal on your Linux box
